Es la prueba fehaciente de que el cambio climático, el calentamiento global, es ya una realidad, una realidad que se ha convertido en una amenaza cuasi existencial, no solo para nuestra forma de vida, sino también para nuestra economía, para nuestros ecosistemas, para nuestra agricultura. Y, por todo ello, llevamos desde el inicio de este mandato trabajando para poner en pie el Pacto Verde Europeo, para hacer una apuesta por las energías renovables, para transformar nuestra economía y disponer de una hoja de ruta para nuestra descarbonización. Y, lamentablemente, durante los últimos meses hemos visto como se ha visto un viraje por parte del Partido Popular Europeo hacia las posiciones negacionistas, que validan argumentos populistas en la extrema derecha, que ponen en riesgo el Pacto Verde Europeo y que, además, prueban una nueva aritmética política en Europa, un nuevo pacto de mayoría entre la derecha y la extrema derecha. Hoy se ha visto derrotada esa estrategia aquí, en el Parlamento Europeo, con la ley de restauración de la naturaleza. Corrijan, porque serán derrotados, y no solo en las urnas, ya que también la historia les acabará juzgando.
translated_text: Madam President, Vice President Timmermans, so far this month we have had the three hottest days ever recorded in human history. They were July 3, 4, and 5. This is clear proof that climate change and global warming are now a reality, a reality that has become an almost existential threat, not only to our way of life, but also to our economy, our ecosystems, and our agriculture. And, for all these reasons, since the beginning of this term of office, we have been working to establish the European Green Deal, to commit to renewable energies, to transform our economy, and to have a roadmap for our decarbonization. Unfortunately, in recent months we have seen a shift by the European People's Party towards denialist positions, which validate populist arguments on the far right, jeopardize the European Green Deal, and, moreover, test a new political arithmetic in Europe, a new majority pact between the right and the far right. Today, that strategy has been defeated here in the European Parliament with the Nature Restoration Law. Correct your course, because you will be defeated, and not only at the polls, but also by history, which will ultimately judge you.

speaker: Grace O’Sullivan
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Madam President, colleagues, there has never been a more significant moment for this House. Today we approved a historic Nature Restoration Law and, with this law, we can protect our oceans, restore our peatlands, and we can make our towns and cities more liveable and healthy places for people and for nature. In the meantime, we must reflect on the campaign that has been waged in this House against the Nature Restoration Law. The leader of the largest political group in the European Parliament has brought his group to the far right. He has overseen a mass campaign of disinformation. And he has lost the respect of this House. It is time for him to reconsider his leadership position. But for now, let’s celebrate a victory for nature restoration, for climate justice, and for honest politics.
translated_text: Madam President, colleagues, there has never been a more significant moment for this House. Today we approved a historic Nature Restoration Law and, with this law, we can protect our oceans, restore our peatlands, and we can make our towns and cities more liveable and healthy places for people and for nature. In the meantime, we must reflect on the campaign that has been waged in this House against the Nature Restoration Law. The leader of the largest political group in the European Parliament has brought his group to the far right. He has overseen a mass campaign of disinformation. And he has lost the respect of this House. It is time for him to reconsider his leadership position. But for now, let’s celebrate a victory for nature restoration, for climate justice, and for honest politics.

speaker: Mazaly Aguilar
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Señora presidente, estimados colegas, cuando se habla en este Pleno del Pacto Verde todo se tiñe de ideología, incluso de una manera imperativa, como cuando el señor Timmermans dice que Europa será verde o no será. Pero vamos a hablar de los hechos: El JRC, el Centro Común de Investigación de la propia Comisión Europea, emitió un informe técnico en el año 2021. Estaba listo en enero, pero hasta agosto no salió; debe de ser que las conclusiones que sacó el informe no les gustaron. Este informe hablaba sobre el Pacto Verde y el impacto de la Estrategia «De la Granja a la Mesa», y en este informe se señalaba, entre otras cosas, que habría un incremento enorme de los costes operativos y que, por tanto, la rentabilidad de las explotaciones se iba a poner en riesgo; que habría un descenso acusado de la producción y, que por tanto, los ingresos de los agricultores seguirían también ese descenso, y que esa falta de producción lo que haría es provocar un incremento indiscriminado de las importaciones de terceros países. Y, a pesar de este demoledor informe, ¿de verdad sigue la Unión Europea pensando en el Pacto Verde? ¿Con una guerra de por medio que ha puesto patas arriba la agricultura en Europa? ¿No entiende la Comisión que es el momento de conseguir la soberanía alimentaria? Nuestro futuro pasa por cuidar el sector primario, no por atacarlo, y la ley de restauración de la naturaleza no lo va a cuidar, sino todo lo contrario.
translated_text: Madam President, dear colleagues, when we talk about the Green Deal in this plenary session, everything becomes tinged with ideology, even in an imperative way, as when Mr. Timmermans says that Europe will be green or it will not be. But let's talk about the facts: the JRC, the European Commission's own Joint Research Center, issued a technical report in 2021. It was ready in January, but it was not released until August; it must be that they did not like the conclusions drawn in the report. This report discussed the Green Deal and the impact of the Farm to Fork Strategy, and this report pointed out, among other things, that there would be a huge increase in operating costs and that, therefore, the profitability of farms would be put at risk; that there would be a sharp decline in production and, therefore, farmers' incomes would also follow that decline, and that this lack of production would lead to an indiscriminate increase in imports from third countries. And despite this devastating report, is the European Union really still considering the Green Deal? With a war raging that has turned agriculture in Europe upside down? Does the Commission not understand that now is the time to achieve food sovereignty? Our future lies in caring for the primary sector, not attacking it, and the nature restoration law will not care for it, quite the contrary.

speaker: Markus Buchheit
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Frau Präsidentin! Ja, Herr Timmermans, Sie werden es nicht wissen, aber ich bin ja ein Menschenfreund, und ich gebe gerne auch mal gratis Wahltipps. Ich glaube, ich habe Ihre Umfrageergebnisse heute gesehen. Wir stehen ja in Deutschland gerade bei 22 %, Sie bei um die sechs, so etwa. Da gebe ich Ihnen gerne meinen Tipp. Und wir reden jetzt gerade darüber, welche Probleme der Grüne Deal in seiner Umsetzung jetzt haben könnte. Der Kollege López hat gerade auch fabuliert, dass es an uns Rechten liegen würde, die hier gerade Probleme verursachen bei der Umsetzung des Grünen Deals. Nein, das ist es eben nicht! Es ist die Lebenswirklichkeit! Meine Damen und Herren, uns ist hier abends ein Programm versprochen worden aus rosaroten Träumen, und aufgewacht sind die Menschen morgens in der Hölle – in der Hölle der Inflation, der Planwirtschaft, der Unfreiheit, der Preissteigerungen und nun heute auch mit einer weiteren Reduzierung der landwirtschaftlichen Anbauflächen, was natürlich zu weiterer Inflation im Lebensmittelbereich führen wird. Meine Damen und Herren, wir müssten uns diesen Realitäten stellen. Es ist Zeit, den Grünen Deal insgesamt infrage zu stellen. Lassen Sie ab davon, und kommen Sie wieder auf hohe Prozente. Ich will Ihnen ja nur helfen.
translated_text: Madam President! Yes, Mr. Timmermans, you may not know this, but I am a philanthropist, and I am happy to give free election advice. I think I saw your poll results today. We are currently at 22% in Germany, and you are at around 6%, more or less. I am happy to give you my tip. And we are currently discussing the problems that the Green Deal might encounter in its implementation. Mr. López has just claimed that it is us on the right who are causing problems with the implementation of the Green Deal. No, that is not the case! It is the reality of life! Ladies and gentlemen, we were promised a program of rosy dreams here this evening, and people woke up this morning in hell – in the hell of inflation, a planned economy, lack of freedom, price increases, and now also with a further reduction in agricultural land, which will of course lead to further inflation in the food sector. Ladies and gentlemen, we must face these realities. It is time to question the Green Deal as a whole. Let go of it and come back to high percentages. I just want to help you.

speaker: Francesca Peppucci
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Signora Presidente, signor Commissario, onorevoli colleghi, il futuro dei nostri figli, dei nostri nipoti, delle future generazioni dipende dalla buona salute del pianeta in cui viviamo. Ne siamo tutti consapevoli, non c'è una forza politica che rispetto ad altre ha più consapevolezza sul vivere in un pianeta sano, nonostante alcuni provino a dimostrare il contrario. Il cambiamento climatico è certamente la più grande sfida che la nostra generazione si trova ad affrontare in termini economici e sociali e dobbiamo essere pronti alle sfide future, lo devono essere i cittadini che sono fuori da questo Parlamento. L'Europa sta realizzando in generale un'agenda positiva sul cambiamento e, per quanto se ne dica, il PPE è sempre stato una forza trainante nella definizione e adozione del green deal, ma mai saremo indifferenti a provvedimenti che mirano a obiettivi comuni a scapito delle comunità e delle imprese, obiettivi poco chiari e realisti nella loro realizzazione. Se infatti per il raggiungimento di un obiettivo che, ripeto essere più che condivisibile, si diventa ciechi e si agisce per ideologia mettendo in difficoltà interi settori, allora proprio quell'obiettivo non solo non sarà compreso dai cittadini europei, ma non sarà nemmeno raggiunto. E la forte contrapposizione che si è creata tra i cittadini in queste settimane ed oggi dovrebbe spingerci, tutti quanti, a un'attenta riflessione sulle politiche da attuare. Le prove future devono essere attuate in maniera intelligente, senza fare demagogia ma soprattutto in maniera concreta.
translated_text: Madam President, Commissioner, ladies and gentlemen, the future of our children, our grandchildren, and future generations depends on the health of the planet we live on. We are all aware of this; no political force is more aware than others of the importance of living on a healthy planet, despite attempts by some to prove otherwise. Climate change is certainly the greatest challenge facing our generation in economic and social terms, and we must be ready for future challenges, as must the citizens outside this Parliament. Europe is generally implementing a positive agenda on change and, whatever may be said, the EPP has always been a driving force in the definition and adoption of the Green Deal, but we will never be indifferent to measures that aim at common goals to the detriment of communities and businesses, goals that are unclear and unrealistic in their implementation. If, in order to achieve an objective that, I repeat, is more than acceptable, we become blind and act on ideology, putting entire sectors in difficulty, then that very objective will not only be misunderstood by European citizens, but will also not be achieved. The strong opposition that has arisen among citizens in recent weeks and today should prompt us all to carefully reflect on the policies to be implemented. Future trials must be carried out intelligently, without demagoguery, but above all in a concrete manner.

speaker: Paul Tang
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Madam President, Commissioner, change needs money and for the biggest change since the Industrial Revolution we need a lot of it – EUR 1 trillion by 2030. Governments can fund part of it, but we need the private sector too. The Commission, including EPP Commissioners have done great work mobilising this, with a definition of sustainability and disclosures for financial products. But one key aspect is still missing: data. Without companies disclosing their impact on people and the planet, investors cannot take this impact into account. A new law was meant to get this data flowing, but right—wing pressure made the Commission backtrack. They gave corporates a get—out—of—jail—free card called materiality assessments. Companies can decide themselves whether something is worth disclosing, and if they don’t, no questions are asked. So I ask the Commission not to undermine sustainable finance, not to undermine the legacy of Ms McGuinness and Mr Dombrovskis and to get corporate reporting going!
translated_text: Madam President, Commissioner, change needs money and for the biggest change since the Industrial Revolution we need a lot of it – EUR 1 trillion by 2030. Governments can fund part of it, but we need the private sector too. The Commission, including EPP Commissioners have done great work mobilising this, with a definition of sustainability and disclosures for financial products. But one key aspect is still missing: data. Without companies disclosing their impact on people and the planet, investors cannot take this impact into account. A new law was meant to get this data flowing, but right—wing pressure made the Commission backtrack. They gave corporates a get—out—of—jail—free card called materiality assessments. Companies can decide themselves whether something is worth disclosing, and if they don’t, no questions are asked. So I ask the Commission not to undermine sustainable finance, not to undermine the legacy of Ms McGuinness and Mr Dombrovskis and to get corporate reporting going!

speaker: Anna Júlia Donáth
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Elnök Asszony! A múlt héten egymás után háromszor is megdőlt a globális melegrekord. Szó szerint a bőrünkön érezzük, hogy mennyire súlyos a baj. De nem csak elszenvedői vagyunk a klímaváltozás következményeinek. Mi, európai döntéshozók felelősek vagyunk azért, hogy megakadályozzuk az élelmiszerellátást veszélyeztető árvizeket és aszályokat. Mi, európai döntéshozók felelősek vagyunk azért, hogy megmentsük Európát a nagyipari kibocsátás szennyeződéseitől és a klímakatasztrófa következményeitől. Éppen ezért nem hagyhatjuk, hogy a szélsőjobboldal - legújabban az Európai Néppárttal karöltve - megakassza a zöld átállás klímavédelmi programját, amit az Európai Parlament jelentős többsége egyértelműen támogat. Ezért arra kérem a Bizottságot, hogy legyen határozottabb és bátrabb a klímaváltozás elleni harcban. Ne hagyja, hogy Európát foglyul ejtsék! Sem azok, akik tagadják a klímaváltozás tényét, sem azok, akik ebből profitálnak.
translated_text: Madam President! Last week, global heat records were broken three times in a row. We can literally feel on our skin how serious the problem is. But we are not just victims of climate change. We, as European decision-makers, are responsible for preventing floods and droughts that threaten food supplies. We, as European decision-makers, are responsible for saving Europe from the pollution caused by large-scale industrial emissions and the consequences of climate catastrophe. That is why we cannot allow the far right – most recently in alliance with the European People's Party – to block the green transition climate protection program, which is clearly supported by a significant majority in the European Parliament. I therefore call on the Commission to be more decisive and courageous in the fight against climate change. Do not let Europe be held hostage! Neither by those who deny the reality of climate change, nor by those who profit from it.

speaker: Martin Häusling
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Frau Präsidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Es ist ein guter Tag heute – ich glaube, da sind sich hier viele einig – nicht nur für die Umwelt, sondern wir können jetzt endlich unsere Verpflichtungen vom Montreal-Abkommen auch umsetzen. Europa ist wieder einmal Vorreiter – hoffentlich. Ich denke, einen Punkt sollten wir hier auch einmal benennen: Die EVP hat ja die Landwirtschaft wieder zum Hauptkampffeld auserkoren, um gegen die Verordnung über die Wiederherstellung der Natur zu kämpfen. Aber die Landwirtschaft ist ja der Hauptbetroffene des Klimawandels. Die Landwirtschaft ist der Hauptbetroffene der Biodiversitätskrise. Wie kann man sich denn solchen Maßnahmen entgegenstellen? Wir müssen Landwirtschaft umbauen, daran geht gar kein Weg vorbei. Wir müssen 50 % Pestizide reduzieren zum Schutz der Biodiversität. Wer hier meint, man könne das nur mit der Gentechnik erreichen, der ist auf einem falschen Weg. Wir müssen agrarökologische Maßnahmen ergreifen, um tatsächlich langfristig Landwirtschaft zu sichern. Und, Herr Timmermans, wir dürfen nicht nachlassen an vielen Punkten. Wir brauchen ein wirklich ambitioniertes Bodenschutzgesetz. Wir brauchen mehr und bessere Tierschutzgesetze in Europa. Ich hoffe, das kommt noch. Wir müssen REACH überarbeiten. Lassen wir uns nicht einschüchtern von der Negativkampagne der EVP. Machen wir ambitioniert weiter mit dem Grünen Deal. Es ist tatsächlich eine der größten Aufgaben der Europäischen Union.
translated_text: Madam President, ladies and gentlemen, today is a good day—I think many here would agree—not only for the environment, but also because we can finally implement our obligations under the Montreal Protocol. Once again, Europe is leading the way—hopefully. I think there is one point we should mention here: the EPP has once again chosen agriculture as its main battleground in its fight against the regulation on nature restoration. But agriculture is the sector most affected by climate change. Agriculture is the sector most affected by the biodiversity crisis. How can anyone oppose such measures? We have to transform agriculture; there is no way around it. We must reduce pesticides by 50% to protect biodiversity. Anyone who thinks that this can only be achieved through genetic engineering is on the wrong track. We must take agroecological measures to actually secure agriculture in the long term. And, Mr. Timmermans, we must not slacken our efforts on many points. We need a truly ambitious soil protection law. We need more and better animal welfare laws in Europe. I hope that will come. We must revise REACH. Let us not be intimidated by the EPP's negative campaign. Let us continue ambitiously with the Green Deal. It is indeed one of the European Union's greatest tasks.

speaker: Grzegorz Tobiszowski
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Pani Przewodnicząca, Panie Komisarzu, Szanowni Państwo! Od 2020 r. mamy kryzys energetyczny spotęgowany atakiem Rosji na Ukrainę, a my w tym czasie podwyższamy cele klimatyczne, generując ogromne koszty dla europejskiej gospodarki. Koszt wprowadzenia pakietu „Fit for 55” będzie o 50% wyższy, niż szacowano w zeszłym roku. Według raportu ekspertów polskiego Banku Pekao S.A. koszt pakietu „Fit for 55” dla Polski, dla mojego kraju, w ciągu najbliższych lat do 2030 r. to ponad 500 mld euro. Eksperci jednoznacznie wskazują, że te koszty już mają i będą miały olbrzymi wpływ na obniżenie poziomu życia Europejczyków i Polaków. Zablokuje to możliwość rozwoju gospodarczego nowych technologii w naszych gospodarkach. Tymczasem kilka dni temu europejski naukowy komitet doradczy ds. zmian klimatu zarekomendował w swoim raporcie, że Unia do 2040 r. powinna zredukować swoje emisje o 90 proc. w odniesieniu do 2090 r. Mam nadzieję, że Komisja nie weźmie pod uwagę tego raportu i tych sugestii.
translated_text: Madam President, Commissioner, ladies and gentlemen, since 2020, we have been experiencing an energy crisis exacerbated by Russia's attack on Ukraine, and at the same time we are raising our climate targets, generating enormous costs for the European economy. The cost of implementing the Fit for 55 package will be 50% higher than estimated last year. According to a report by experts from the Polish bank Pekao S.A., the cost of the Fit for 55 package for Poland, my country, over the next few years until 2030 will be over EUR 500 billion. Experts clearly indicate that these costs already have and will continue to have a huge impact on lowering the standard of living of Europeans and Poles. This will block the possibility of economic development of new technologies in our economies. Meanwhile, a few days ago, the European Scientific Advisory Committee on Climate Change recommended in its report that the EU should reduce its emissions by 90% by 2040 compared to 2090. I hope that the Commission will not take this report and these suggestions into account.

speaker: Sylvia Limmer
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Frau Präsidentin! Alle sollen im grün-sozialistischen Gleichschritt dem Grüner-Deal-Verbotsmarathon zustimmen. Andernfalls fehle laut Herrn Timmermans die nötige Solidarität, um den Rest der Welt zum gemeinsamen grünen Suizid zu zwingen. Selbst der vorgebliche Widerstand von EVP-Chef Manfred Weber gegen die Pläne der EU-Kommissionspräsidentin mit einem CDU-Parteibuch war nur formaler Natur. Die EVP sei die Partei des Grünen Deals, hieß es heute. Lediglich aus Sorge vor Mehrheitsverlusten wolle man die Bürger lieber etwas langsamer grün kochen, damit sie nicht wie der berüchtigte Frosch im Topf vorzeitig die Flucht ergreifen. Sie alle in Ihrer grandiosen Brüsseler Abgehobenheit übersehen, dass immer mehr Bürger völlig zu Recht ihre Verbots-, Enteignungs- und Entmündigungspolitik mit fast totalitär anmutenden Einschränkungen in allen Lebensbereichen gründlich satthaben. Sie wollen vorgeblich das Klima schützen, aber Ihre Grüner-Deal-Angstideologie ist zutiefst freiheits-, demokratie- und bürgerfeindlich.
translated_text: Madam President! Everyone should agree to the Green Deal ban marathon in green-socialist lockstep. Otherwise, according to Mr. Timmermans, there will be a lack of solidarity needed to force the rest of the world into collective green suicide. Even the supposed resistance of EPP leader Manfred Weber, a member of the CDU, to the plans of the EU Commission President was only formal in nature. The EPP is the party of the Green Deal, it was said today. It is only out of concern for losing the majority that they want to cook citizens green a little more slowly, so that they do not flee prematurely like the infamous frog in the pot. In your grandiose Brussels aloofness, you all overlook the fact that more and more citizens are completely fed up with your policies of prohibition, expropriation, and disenfranchisement, with almost totalitarian restrictions in all areas of life. You claim to want to protect the climate, but your Green Deal fearmongering ideology is deeply hostile to freedom, democracy, and citizens.

speaker: Juan Ignacio Zoido Álvarez
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Señora presidenta, señor Timmermans, ayer centenares de agricultores y ganaderos venidos de toda Europa se manifestaban a las puertas de este Parlamento. No pedían ayudas, no pedían financiación, ni tan siquiera pedían leyes favorables al campo. No, pedían sencillamente que no les impusiéramos más restricciones y les dejáramos hacer aquello que saben hacer: producir alimentos de calidad a precios razonables para todos los europeos. En apenas tres años han tenido que enfrentarse a una pandemia, a una guerra, a una inflación disparada y a una de las sequías más graves de las últimas décadas. Pero todavía hay algunos que creen que debemos añadirles más cargas, que debemos pedirles más esfuerzo. Algunos como los eurodiputados socialistas, que han decidido ignorarles y apoyar la ley de restauración de la naturaleza. Mientras los agricultores y los ganaderos, afuera, temían por su futuro y temían por su subsistencia económica, los diputados socialistas, aquí dentro, aplaudían y daban gritos de alegría. Si lo que pretenden es alejar al campo de las instituciones europeas y dar aire y alas al populismo, desde luego van por el buen camino. Señor Timmermans, termino. Usted está convirtiendo el Pacto Verde en un pacto negro para el sector primario y el mundo rural. Tenemos que seguir apostando por la lucha contra el cambio climático y defendiendo la sostenibilidad medioambiental. Claro que sí. Pero no podemos hacerlo a costa de nadie. Necesitamos una política medioambiental de consenso que suponga una oportunidad de crecimiento para todos los europeos. También para la Europa rural. (El orador se niega a que Petros Kokkalis le formule una pregunta con arreglo al procedimiento de la «tarjeta azul»).
translated_text: Madam President, Mr. Timmermans, yesterday hundreds of farmers and ranchers from all over Europe demonstrated outside this Parliament. They were not asking for aid, they were not asking for funding, they were not even asking for laws favorable to the countryside. No, they were simply asking that we not impose more restrictions on them and that we let them do what they know how to do: produce quality food at reasonable prices for all Europeans. In just three years, they have had to face a pandemic, a war, skyrocketing inflation, and one of the worst droughts in decades. But there are still some who believe that we should add more burdens to them, that we should ask them to make more effort. Some, such as the socialist MEPs, have decided to ignore them and support the nature restoration law. While farmers and ranchers outside feared for their future and their economic survival, socialist MEPs inside applauded and shouted with joy. If their aim is to alienate the countryside from the European institutions and give wings to populism, they are certainly on the right track. Mr. Timmermans, I will conclude. You are turning the Green Deal into a black deal for the primary sector and the rural world. We must continue to commit ourselves to the fight against climate change and to defending environmental sustainability. Of course we do. But we cannot do so at anyone's expense. We need a consensus-based environmental policy that provides an opportunity for growth for all Europeans. Including rural Europe. (The speaker refuses to take a question from Petros Kokkalis under the 'blue card' procedure).

speaker: Cyrus Engerer
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Sinjura President, illum kienet ġurnata tajba ħafna għaċ-ċittadini Ewropej però jidher li ma kinitx ġurnata tajba għall-Partit Popolari Ewropew, għaliex illum ħareġ b’mod ċar kif il-politika tan-Nazzjonalisti hi bbażata biss fuq greenwashing. Jgħidu li għandhom qalbhom mal-ambjent, imma f’kull ċans illi jkollhom jippruvaw idgħajfu l-liġijiet illi jipproteġu l-ambjent tagħna, idgħajfu l-ġlieda kontra t-tibdil fil-klima u allura jdgħajfu l-protezzjoni tas-saħħa tagħna l-bnedmin. Għaliex min verament jemmen fit-tisħiħ tan-natura tagħna, ma jivvutax emenda wara oħra biex idgħajjef liġijiet bħal ta’ dalgħodu. Rajna kif quddiem vot daqstant kruċjali għall-futur tal-ambjent tagħna, in-Nazzjonalisti kienu maqsumin, ta’ Weber kontra ta’ Metsola, ta’ Metsola kontra ta’ Von der Leyen, fejn saħansittra, il-President ta’ dan il-Parlament, kienet lesta li tinża’ l-kappell newtrali tagħha, li tant tiftaħar bih, u titkellem b’mod partiġġjan fuq il-Financial Times kontra, u nikwota, “... li nimponu ambizzjonijiet ambjentali fuq iċ-ċittadini tagħna”. Li għadhom ma fehmux hu li aħna maċ-ċittadini tkellimna u se nkomplu nitkellmu. Irridu l-ikbar protezzjoni possibbli għall-ambjent tagħna u għal saħħitna.
translated_text: Madam President, today was a very good day for European citizens, but it seems it was not a good day for the European People's Party, because today it became clear how the Nationalists' policy is based solely on greenwashing. They say they have their hearts with the environment, but at every opportunity they have to try to weaken the laws that protect our environment, they weaken the fight against climate change and thus weaken the protection of our human health. Because anyone who truly believes in strengthening our nature would not vote for one amendment after another to weaken laws like the one this morning. We saw how, in front of such a crucial vote for the future of our environment, the Nationalists were divided, with Weber against Metsola, with Metsola versus Von der Leyen, where even the President of this Parliament was ready to take off her neutral hat, which she is so proud of, and speak in a partisan manner against, and I quote, "…imposing environmental ambitions on our citizens." What they still don't understand is that we have spoken with the citizens and we will continue to speak. We want the greatest possible protection for our environment and for our health.

speaker: Ulrike Müller
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Frau Präsidentin, sehr geehrte Kollegen, sehr geehrter Herr Vizepräsident Timmermans! Ich habe den Grünen Deal von Anfang an begrüßt. Wir müssen aber aus der Transformation der Wirtschaft eine Wachstumsstrategie machen. Daran hängt nämlich unser künftiger Wohlstand. Was wir aber haben, ist ein Puzzle mit 1000 Teilen ohne Vorlage – eine schwierige Angelegenheit. Ihr Job ist es, die Puzzleteile passend zu machen. Leider passt hier absolut nichts mehr zusammen. Dadurch droht allen wichtigen Akteuren Überlastung. Das Verständnis für die EU-Politik geht verloren. Unternehmen droht immer mehr Bürokratie – Stichworte Lieferketten und Industrieemissionen. Die Landwirte haben wir längst verloren – Stichworte SUR und NRL. Und auch viele Bürger drohen wir zu verlieren. Den Verlust von Industrieplätzen werden sie nicht vergeben, und der deutsche Heizungsstreit gibt einen Vorgeschmack auf die Umsetzung der Gebäudeenergieeffizienz-Richtlinie. Wenn der Grüne Deal die Menschen vergisst, bekommen wir die Rechnung sehr bald.
translated_text: Madam President, colleagues, Vice President Timmermans, I have welcomed the Green Deal from the outset. However, we must turn the transformation of the economy into a growth strategy. Our future prosperity depends on it. What we have, however, is a 1,000-piece jigsaw puzzle without a template—a difficult task. Your job is to make the pieces of the puzzle fit together. Unfortunately, nothing fits together anymore. This threatens to overwhelm all the key players. Understanding of EU policy is being lost. Companies are facing increasing bureaucracy—keywords: supply chains and industrial emissions. We lost farmers long ago—keywords: SUR and NRL. And we are also in danger of losing many citizens. They will not forgive the loss of industrial jobs, and the German heating dispute gives a foretaste of the implementation of the Energy Performance of Buildings Directive. If the Green Deal forgets the people, we will pay the price very soon.

speaker: Francisco Guerreiro
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Senhora Presidente, Senhor Comissário, estamos a falar aqui do caminho de transição para a descarbonização e creio que é fundamental falarmos sobre agricultura, porque este tema e esta área foram usados como arma de arremesso para esta transição e nós vimos isto na Nature Restoration Law. E é fundamental quebrar aqui alguns mitos. Nós não temos propriamente falta de produção alimentar. Aliás, cerca de 20% do que produzimos é desperdiçado e, portanto, temos que dar um enfoque ao desperdício alimentar e garantir não só que estes bens alimentares são direcionados para quem mais precisa, mas também que os pequenos produtores, muitos deles que não são beneficiados nesta cadeia alimentar, também tenham o rendimento que necessitam. Mas depois também falar que (e vai ser apresentada uma proposta da Comissão, em breve, sobre o sistema alimentar saudável) que é importante nós também caminharmos para um sistema mais plant based, portanto, com menos produção animal, mais extensivo, mais biológico, que efetivamente seja mais resiliente e que garanta melhor rendimento não só a quem produz alimentos, mas depois também uma política pública de saúde que é: bens alimentares saudáveis, mais próximos, mais saudáveis, mais locais e, portanto, mais plant based e com menos proteína animal. Portanto, é fundamental essa proposta que vem da Comissão e estaremos cá para debatê—la e para cumprir o Pacto Ecológico Europeu.
translated_text: Madam President, Commissioner, we are talking here about the transition to decarbonization, and I believe it is essential to talk about agriculture, because this topic and this area have been used as a weapon for this transition, and we have seen this in the Nature Restoration Law. It is essential to dispel some myths here. We do not exactly have a shortage of food production. In fact, around 20% of what we produce is wasted, so we need to focus on food waste and ensure not only that these foodstuffs are directed to those who need them most, but also that small producers, many of whom do not benefit from this food chain, also have the income they need. But then we also need to mention (and a Commission proposal on the healthy food system will be presented shortly) that it is important for us to move towards a more plant-based system, with less animal production, more extensive, more organic, which is effectively more resilient and guarantees better income not only for those who produce food, but also a public health policy that is: healthy food, closer, healthier, more local and, therefore, more plant-based and with less animal protein. Therefore, this proposal from the Commission is essential, and we will be here to debate it and to comply with the European Green Deal.

speaker: Johan Van Overtveldt
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Voorzitter, commissaris, de duurzame transitie en het terugdringen van koolstofemissies zijn een noodzaak. Extreme standpunten of benaderingen, laat staan dogma’s – van welke zijde dan ook – helpen de realisatie van die doelstellingen niet vooruit. Het beleid rond de Green Deal focust te veel op ideologie. We moeten ons vaker de vraag durven stellen wat daarmee bereikt wordt en of er nog meer regelgeving nodig is om de nieuwe uitdagingen aan te kunnen. Dit debat smeekt al vanaf de startfase om een realistische aanpak. Een op data en wetenschap gebaseerde transitie die onze welvaart en koopkracht in stand kan houden en zelfs versterken. Geen torenhoge ambities die investeerders, bedrijven en de samenleving afschrikken en onzekerheid creëren. Realisme. Het komt erop aan om samen met alle betrokken partijen, ook met de industrie, te zorgen voor een ondernemings- en investeringsklimaat dat innoveren beloont zonder te verdrinken in regelgeving. En dit alles op een realistische manier met een haalbare timing.
translated_text: Chair, Commissioner, the sustainable transition and the reduction of carbon emissions are a necessity. Extreme positions or approaches, let alone dogmas—from whichever side—do not help to achieve those objectives. The policy surrounding the Green Deal focuses too much on ideology. We must dare to ask ourselves more often what this achieves and whether more regulation is needed to meet the new challenges. From the outset, this debate has called for a realistic approach. A transition based on data and science that can maintain and even strengthen our prosperity and purchasing power. Not sky-high ambitions that scare off investors, businesses, and society and create uncertainty. Realism. It is important to work with all stakeholders, including industry, to ensure a business and investment climate that rewards innovation without drowning in regulation. And all this in a realistic way with a feasible timeline.

speaker: Philippe Olivier
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Madame la Présidente, mes chers collègues, personne ici n’est indifférent au sort de la planète et chacun a conscience de la nécessité de réformer un système fondé sur une consommation débridée. L’Europe, l’Union européenne se veut être le meilleur élève de la classe planétaire. Elle s’est lancée avec le pacte vert dans une course dont les écologistes radicaux ont fixé les règles, les étapes et les objectifs. Votre démarche n’est pas pragmatique, mais idéologique. On est passé de la croissance durable à la croissance zéro, puis maintenant à la décroissance. Décroissance énergétique, décroissance industrielle. Et au moment où l’humanité a besoin de nourrir des milliards d’êtres humains, décroissance agricole. Vous avez fait de la malheureuse Hollande le laboratoire de votre projet de destruction des élevages. Un projet largement inspiré de l’idéologie antispéciste qui est une idéologie antihumaniste. Le vote de ce matin constitue un avertissement dont vous devez tenir compte. À défaut, vous verrez les peuples, loin de vous suivre dans vos folies écologistes, se rebeller pour restaurer l’Europe, un continent qui croit en la science et en l’avenir.
translated_text: Madam President, ladies and gentlemen, no one here is indifferent to the fate of the planet, and everyone is aware of the need to reform a system based on unbridled consumption. Europe, the European Union, wants to be the best student in the global classroom. With the Green Deal, it has embarked on a race whose rules, stages, and objectives have been set by radical environmentalists. Your approach is not pragmatic, but ideological. We have moved from sustainable growth to zero growth, and now to degrowth. Energy degrowth, industrial degrowth. And at a time when humanity needs to feed billions of people, agricultural degrowth. You have turned unfortunate Holland into a laboratory for your project to destroy livestock farming. A project largely inspired by anti-speciesist ideology, which is an anti-humanist ideology. This morning's vote is a warning that you must heed. Otherwise, you will see the people, far from following you in your environmentalist follies, rebel to restore Europe, a continent that believes in science and in the future.

speaker: Ljudmila Novak
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Spoštovani predsedujoča, gospod komisar, kolegice in kolegi. Eden izmed dejavnikov tveganja za neuspešnost zelenega prehoda je tudi strah. Strah pred neznanim. Strah, da je Evropa pri zelenih prizadevanjih v svetu osamljena. Strah, da bo Evropa postala nekonkurenčna in se bo zmanjšalo blagostanje naših ljudi. Strah politikov pred izgubo glasov. Zaradi tega strahu pride do neustreznega izvajanja politik, do odpora nekaterih industrij, kmetov ali držav članic. Ljudje mislijo, da si te ukrepe izmišljajo bruseljski birokrati, ki ne poznajo realnega življenja. Za soočenje s temi strahovi je pomembno poslušati ljudi, slišati njihove pomisleke, iskati razumne rešitve ter pojasnjevati razloge in prednosti. Potrebni so pogum, sodelovanje in vizija, kot so jo imeli ustanovni očetje Evropske unije. Sedaj moramo premagati strahove in združiti moči pri uresničevanju zelenega prehoda. Čas pa bo pokazal, ali so ti strahovi upravičeni. Ali so naši ukrepi pravi in učinkoviti. In ne današnje glasovanje.
translated_text: Madam President, Commissioner, colleagues. One of the risk factors for the failure of the green transition is fear. Fear of the unknown. Fear that Europe is isolated in its green efforts in the world. Fear that Europe will become uncompetitive and that the prosperity of our people will decline. Fear among politicians of losing votes. This fear leads to the inadequate implementation of policies and to resistance from certain industries, farmers, or Member States. People think that these measures are being devised by Brussels bureaucrats who are out of touch with real life. To address these fears, it is important to listen to people, hear their concerns, seek reasonable solutions, and explain the reasons and benefits. We need courage, cooperation, and vision, just like the founding fathers of the European Union had. Now we have to overcome our fears and join forces to make the green transition happen. Time will tell if these fears are justified. If our actions are right and effective. Not today's vote.

speaker: Цветелина Пенкова
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Г-жо Председател, осъществяването на целите на зелената сделка не може да се реализира без промяна в икономическия модел на Европейския съюз, но това не трябва да става чрез задълбочаване на неравенствата между държавите членки и създаване на рискове производствата на чисти технологии да се концентрират само в някои определени и по-богати държави. Основен приоритет на работата ни по законодателния танк за промишленост с нулеви нетни емисии е да осигурим нужното европейско финансиране, да създадем повече високоплатени и устойчиви работни места. Този регламент трябва да бъде приет възможно най-скоро. Енергийната криза ни показа, че развитието на възобновяеми източници на енергия няма да бъде достатъчно, за да задоволим енергийните нужди на Европа. Имаме нужда от модернизиране и развитие и на базовите енергийни източници, включително и ядрените такива. Трябва да продължим да развиваме отраслите, в които нашите компании са глобални лидери. Ще имаме нужда от много висококвалифицирани кадри. Тяхната подготовка трябва да започне от вчера, а не от утре.
translated_text: Madam President, the objectives of the Green Deal cannot be achieved without a change in the economic model of the European Union, but this should not be done by deepening inequalities between Member States and creating risks that clean technology production will be concentrated only in certain, wealthier countries. The main priority of our work on the legislative tank for net-zero emissions industry is to secure the necessary European funding and create more high-paying and sustainable jobs. This regulation must be adopted as soon as possible. The energy crisis has shown us that the development of renewable energy sources will not be enough to meet Europe's energy needs. We also need to modernize and develop our basic energy sources, including nuclear energy. We must continue to develop the industries in which our companies are global leaders. We will need a large number of highly qualified personnel. Their training must begin yesterday, not tomorrow.

speaker: Barry Andrews
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Madam President, it must be very hard to be a conservative during an emergency. The EPP has not really covered itself in glory over the last few weeks, with its very obvious attempts to kill the Nature Restoration Law, with its infantile tweets and intimidation of MEPs. The Parliament sent out a very clear message today, and a very important message, a shot that was heard around the world. The message is that we can deliver for nature restoration and biodiversity. We can do the right thing by our citizens, as we have done so many times with the Green Deal in all its parts. We can pass into law the will of the people, who voted for environmental considerations in 2019. We can deliver on the Green Deal and most importantly, we can do it without the EPP. It is clear that a climate emergency is not a place for conservatives.
translated_text: Madam President, it must be very hard to be a conservative during an emergency. The EPP has not really covered itself in glory over the last few weeks, with its very obvious attempts to kill the Nature Restoration Law, with its infantile tweets and intimidation of MEPs. The Parliament sent out a very clear message today, and a very important message, a shot that was heard around the world. The message is that we can deliver for nature restoration and biodiversity. We can do the right thing by our citizens, as we have done so many times with the Green Deal in all its parts. We can pass into law the will of the people, who voted for environmental considerations in 2019. We can deliver on the Green Deal and most importantly, we can do it without the EPP. It is clear that a climate emergency is not a place for conservatives.

speaker: Zdzisław Krasnodębski
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Pani Przewodnicząca! Panie Przewodniczący! Jak pokazuje ta debata, jesteśmy w trochę innej fazie budowy zielonego ładu. Zaczęliśmy przekładać ogólne ceny na konkretne decyzje legislacyjne, a ludzie zaczęli odczuwać skutki. Okazało się –co niby wszyscy wiedzieliśmy, ale tylko teoretycznie – że transformacja energetyczna i polityka klimatyczna będą kosztowne i będą łączyć się z wyrzeczeniami obywateli. Tak długo jak koszty transformacji miał płacić np. polski górnik, a nie duński, holenderski czy niemiecki rolnik, nie budziło to państwa niepokoju politycznego. Ale dziś okazuje się, że znaczna część wyborców w Niemczech, we Francji, w Danii czy w Niderlandach nie jest gotowa do tych wyrzeczeń. Nie chce np. zrezygnować z samochodu z silnikiem spalinowym, zapłacić za wymianę piecyka gazowego, ograniczyć spożycie i zmieniać swoje preferencje wyborcze. Dlatego niektórzy koledzy wykazują znacznie mniej entuzjazmu wobec zielonego ładu i odkrywają jego ideologiczność. Należy oczekiwać, że w przyszłości ta tendencja się pogłębi i może obejmie również inne grupy polityczne.
translated_text: Madam President, Mr. President, as this debate shows, we are in a slightly different phase of building a green deal. We have begun to translate general prices into specific legislative decisions, and people have begun to feel the effects. It has turned out – as we all knew, but only in theory – that the energy transition and climate policy will be costly and will involve sacrifices on the part of citizens. As long as the costs of the transition were to be paid by, for example, Polish miners, and not Danish, Dutch, or German farmers, this did not cause you any political concern. But today, it turns out that a significant proportion of voters in Germany, France, Denmark, and the Netherlands are not prepared to make these sacrifices. They do not want to give up their combustion engine cars, pay for the replacement of their gas stoves, reduce their consumption, or change their voting preferences. That is why some colleagues are much less enthusiastic about the green deal and are discovering its ideological nature. It is to be expected that this trend will intensify in the future and may also spread to other political groups.

speaker: Tomislav Sokol
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Poštovana predsjedavajuća, težnja da Europa postane prvi klimatski neutralan kontinent ne smije se pretvoriti u ekstremističku zelenu agendu koja gospodarski unazađuje Europsku uniju. Nažalost, podaci jasno pokazuju da je Europa u procesu deindustrijalizacije. U dramatičnim geopolitičkim okolnostima EU gubi utrku konkurentnosti na globalnoj razini, ponajprije zbog silnih regulatornih ograničenja koja sve više opterećuju europske poljoprivrednike, ribare i industriju i odvlače investicije u druge dijelove svijeta. Ako ovako nastavimo, uništit ćemo europsku civilizaciju koja se gradila stoljećima. Osim toga, ako zbog implementacije ovakvog Europskog zelenog plana proizvodnja bude preseljena u države s nižim okolišnim standardima od onih u Europskoj uniji, ne samo da nećemo smanjiti emisije nego ćemo ih na kraju povećati. Iluzija je očekivati da će proizvođači u trećim državama u dogledno vrijeme poštovati visoke ekološke standarde koji vrijede u Europskoj uniji. Dakle, u interesu je same zaštite okoliša zadržati proizvodnju unutar EU. Kolegice i kolege, u okolnostima u kojima je Europska unija odgovorna za manje od devet posto svjetskih emisija CO2, glavno rješenje za globalno smanjenje emisija ne može biti u deindustrijalizaciji i uništenju europskog načina života.
translated_text: Dear Chair, the aspiration for Europe to become the first climate-neutral continent must not be turned into an extremist green agenda that economically sets back the European Union. Unfortunately, the data clearly show that Europe is in the process of deindustrialization. In these dramatic geopolitical circumstances, the EU is losing the global competitiveness race, primarily because of the massive regulatory burdens that increasingly weigh down European farmers, fishermen, and industry, and divert investment to other parts of the world. If we continue like this, we will destroy European civilization, which has been built over centuries. Furthermore, if the implementation of such a European Green Deal causes production to be moved to countries with lower environmental standards than those in the European Union, we will not only fail to reduce emissions but will ultimately increase them. It is an illusion to expect that manufacturers in third countries will adhere to the high environmental standards of the European Union anytime soon. Therefore, in the interest of environmental protection itself, we must keep production within the EU. Colleagues, in circumstances where the European Union is responsible for less than nine percent of the world's CO2 emissions, the main solution for global emissions reduction cannot be deindustrialization and the destruction of the European way of life.

speaker: Milan Brglez
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Predsedujoča, hvala za besedo. Spoštovani komisar, kolegice in kolegi. Sprejetje evropskega zelenega dogovora je potrdilo novo evropsko družbeno pogodbo. Našega spopadanja s podnebnimi in okoljskimi izzivi. Za njeno uresničevanje bo osrednja vloga gospodarstva, ki mora služiti – prvič ali pa predvsem – interesom družbe in okolja in šele potem kapitalu. Osnovni predpogoj preobrazbe v pravično in vključujočo družbo je združevanje vseh deležnikov v vsej njihovi raznolikosti. To bo omogočilo trajnostni in družbeno odgovorni gospodarski razvoj za zaščito dobrobiti in zdravja ljudi. Zdravstvena in energetska kriza sta poglobili socialno stisko najranljivejših posameznikov in skupin. Zato moramo več storiti v smeri vzdržnostnega ali trajnostnega razvoja. Torej zdaj potrebujemo zadostno mero politične treznosti, odločnosti in drznosti, da bodoče generacije ne bodo združene v pomanjkanju, ampak v raznolikosti.
translated_text: Chair, thank you for giving me the floor. Commissioner, colleagues. The adoption of the European Green Deal has confirmed a new European social contract. Our response to climate and environmental challenges. The economy will play a central role in its implementation, serving first and foremost the interests of society and the environment, and only then capital. The basic prerequisite for the transformation into a fair and inclusive society is the unification of all stakeholders in all their diversity. This will enable sustainable and socially responsible economic development to protect people's well-being and health. The health and energy crises have exacerbated the social distress of the most vulnerable individuals and groups. We must therefore do more to promote sustainable development. So now we need a sufficient degree of political sobriety, determination, and boldness so that future generations will not be united in deprivation, but in diversity.

speaker: Hermann Tertsch
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Señora presidente, señor comisario, este es un Pacto Verde que dicen que va despacio. El Pacto Verde es realmente un plan ideológico de ingeniería social contra los intereses de los pueblos europeos. Los pueblos europeos están sufriendo ya gravemente los efectos de ese Pacto Verde. La arrogancia totalitaria de estos ideólogos que desde aquí quieren imponer unos ritmos y unas condiciones a la gente que no está preparada, que no puede —y muchas de esas condiciones son absoluta y netamente negativas—, es un auténtico drama y desautoriza a este Parlamento. Hoy es un día de desgracia, porque esa ley de la restauración, que demuestra simplemente que hay unos intereses ideológicos y materiales —también materiales— totalmente contrarios a los de los pueblos europeos, nos va a llevar a la miseria y a la desaparición del sector primario en Europa. Y eso es al parecer lo que lo que se quiere. En Europa necesitamos realmente que volvamos al sentido común, y junio del año 2024 va a ser cuando los pueblos de Europa, los intereses de Europa, se van a levantar contra este delirio verde del Pacto y de la restauración de la naturaleza.
translated_text: Madam President, Commissioner, this is a Green Deal that they say is moving slowly. The Green Deal is really an ideological plan of social engineering against the interests of the European people. The European people are already suffering severely from the effects of this Green Deal. The totalitarian arrogance of these ideologues who want to impose rhythms and conditions on people who are not prepared, who cannot cope—and many of these conditions are absolutely and clearly negative—is a real tragedy and undermines the authority of this Parliament. Today is a day of misfortune, because this restoration law, which simply demonstrates that there are ideological and material interests—material interests too—that are totally contrary to those of the European people, will lead us to misery and the disappearance of the primary sector in Europe. And that seems to be what is wanted. In Europe, we really need to return to common sense, and June 2024 will be when the peoples of Europe, the interests of Europe, will rise up against this green delirium of the Pact and the restoration of nature.

speaker: Seán Kelly
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: A Uachtaráin, Commissioner, climate change is flexing its muscles in the form of floods, droughts, tornadoes and hurricanes, and is striking indiscriminately. The threat of climate change requires changes in the ways in which we produce, consume and organise our societies. Having said that, I am very pleased with the outcome of today’s vote, which is a positive one for nature restoration. Aimed at protecting natural resources, the law is a fundamental pillar of the Green Deal. Today’s vote now gives us the opportunity to improve the text of the Commission in line with what we need to make it work for everybody. Over—intensive land use, climate change, pollution, over—exploitation and other human pressures are rapidly degrading our natural resources. We know we need to act. We must ensure that the path we take is ambitious, but it must also be rooted in practicality. Hopefully the outcome of the trilogues will achieve this.
translated_text: A Uachtaráin, Commissioner, climate change is flexing its muscles in the form of floods, droughts, tornadoes and hurricanes, and is striking indiscriminately. The threat of climate change requires changes in the ways in which we produce, consume and organise our societies. Having said that, I am very pleased with the outcome of today’s vote, which is a positive one for nature restoration. Aimed at protecting natural resources, the law is a fundamental pillar of the Green Deal. Today’s vote now gives us the opportunity to improve the text of the Commission in line with what we need to make it work for everybody. Over—intensive land use, climate change, pollution, over—exploitation and other human pressures are rapidly degrading our natural resources. We know we need to act. We must ensure that the path we take is ambitious, but it must also be rooted in practicality. Hopefully the outcome of the trilogues will achieve this.

speaker: Christophe Clergeau
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Madame la Présidente, cher Frans Timmermans, grâce au vote de ce matin, le pacte vert va de l’avant et c’est une bonne nouvelle. Maintenant, nous avons besoin d’un pacte vert agricole. La situation de blocage actuel ne peut pas continuer. Ce matin, tout le volet agricole de la loi a été repoussé et ce n’est pas une bonne nouvelle. Le monde agricole ne doit plus repousser les changements, mais s’y engager totalement, car son avenir en dépend. Nous avons de notre côté l’obligation d’utiliser tous les moyens, y compris financiers – et vous l’avez évoqué – pour les accompagner. Et ce matin, la droite a rejeté toute mobilisation de la PAC et tout nouvel outil financier. Alors je pose ce soir la question: comment rouvrir la discussion? Comment reconstruire la confiance? Comment se donner les moyens d’un pacte vert agricole? Je suis pour ma part convaincu que cela passe par une nouvelle politique agricole et alimentaire commune, radicalement différente de l’actuelle, pour restaurer la nature et assurer la souveraineté alimentaire de l’Europe. Catch-the-eye procedure
translated_text: Madam President, dear Frans Timmermans, thanks to this morning's vote, the Green Deal is moving forward, which is good news. Now we need a Green Deal for agriculture. The current deadlock cannot continue. This morning, the entire agricultural section of the law was postponed, which is not good news. The agricultural sector must no longer resist change, but must commit fully to it, because its future depends on it. For our part, we have an obligation to use all means, including financial ones—as you mentioned—to support them. And this morning, the right wing rejected any mobilization of the CAP and any new financial tools. So tonight I ask the question: how can we reopen the discussion? How can we rebuild trust? How can we give ourselves the means to achieve a green agricultural pact? For my part, I am convinced that this requires a new common agricultural and food policy, radically different from the current one, in order to restore nature and ensure Europe's food sovereignty. Catch-the-eye procedure

speaker: Susanna Ceccardi
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario Timmermans, io mi chiedo come mai stiamo portando avanti in Europa politiche così masochistiche che penalizzano i posti di lavoro dei cittadini europei? Io vengo dalla Toscana, abito vicino Pontedera. A Pontedera c'è lo stabilimento della Piaggio, dove viene prodotta la mitica Vespa. Ecco, hanno iniziato a produrre la Vespa elettrica. Sono andati dei visitatori a visitare lo stabilimento e nella linea di produzione della Vespa elettrica non c'era quasi nessuno. Ma saranno in sciopero? No, non sono in sciopero, è che per l'elettrico serve, nella linea di produzione, un terzo degli operai che servirebbero per il motore a scoppio. Tra l'altro il motore a scoppio è nato proprio in Toscana, a Pietrasanta. Io mi chiedo cosa penserebbero i nostri grandi imprenditori della storia – Enzo Ferrari, Ferruccio Lamborghini, Enrico Piaggio – che hanno creato milioni di posti di lavoro insieme a tanti altri imprenditori europei, che in questo hanno fatto la storia dell'economia mondiale ed europea? Loro creavano posti di lavoro, Lei, che appartiene anche al Partito dei lavoratori, dovrebbe cambiare partito, perché voi create disoccupati!
translated_text: Madam President, colleagues, Commissioner Timmermans, I wonder why we are pursuing such masochistic policies in Europe that penalize the jobs of European citizens. I come from Tuscany, I live near Pontedera. In Pontedera, there is the Piaggio factory, where the legendary Vespa is produced. They have started producing the electric Vespa. Some visitors went to visit the factory and there was hardly anyone on the electric Vespa production line. Are they on strike? No, they are not on strike, it is just that the electric production line requires a third of the workers needed for the combustion engine. Incidentally, the combustion engine was invented in Tuscany, in Pietrasanta. I wonder what our great entrepreneurs of history—Enzo Ferrari, Ferruccio Lamborghini, Enrico Piaggio—who created millions of jobs together with many other European entrepreneurs, who made history in the global and European economy, would think about this? They created jobs. You, who also belong to the Workers' Party, should change parties, because you are creating unemployment!

speaker: Mick Wallace
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Madam President, I would like to commend the Commissioner for the good work on the Nature Restoration Law. God knows it’s not easy making progress on climate and environmental ambition in this place. To start, the 55% reduction target in the EU climate law that Parliament passed two years ago is too low given the EU’s historical contribution to global rising temperatures and the EU’s capacity to reduce its emissions. Global temperature simply cannot be limited to a 1.5 degree increase if this remains the ceiling of the EU’s ambition. Today we passed the Nature Restoration Law. I am delighted that the regulation lives to fight another day. In that sense, it’s a good day for the people of the EU, for nature and for farmers in particular. But the text passed by Parliament today, as you know, has been gutted. It’s a shell of the Commission’s proposal. Parliament voted to delete the entire article on agricultural ecosystems. It voted to restrict restoration measures for terrestrial ecosystems to Natura 2000 protected areas only. Let’s hope we can fix some of these problems in trilogue. It will be a big challenge. (End of catch-the-eye procedure)
translated_text: Madam President, I would like to commend the Commissioner for the good work on the Nature Restoration Law. God knows it’s not easy making progress on climate and environmental ambition in this place. To start, the 55% reduction target in the EU climate law that Parliament passed two years ago is too low given the EU’s historical contribution to global rising temperatures and the EU’s capacity to reduce its emissions. Global temperature simply cannot be limited to a 1.5 degree increase if this remains the ceiling of the EU’s ambition. Today we passed the Nature Restoration Law. I am delighted that the regulation lives to fight another day. In that sense, it’s a good day for the people of the EU, for nature and for farmers in particular. But the text passed by Parliament today, as you know, has been gutted. It’s a shell of the Commission’s proposal. Parliament voted to delete the entire article on agricultural ecosystems. It voted to restrict restoration measures for terrestrial ecosystems to Natura 2000 protected areas only. Let’s hope we can fix some of these problems in trilogue. It will be a big challenge. (End of catch-the-eye procedure)

speaker: Estrella Durá Ferrandis
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: El cambio climático y la degradación del medio ambiente son las mayores amenazas para nuestro bienestar y nuestra existencia. Dada la situación de emergencia climática en la que nos encontramos, el grupo socialista ha estado desde un principio comprometido con el Pacto Verde Europeo y creemos que es la única estrategia de desarrollo posible para garantizar un futuro digno. Medidas como la Ley Europea del Clima o el paquete «Objetivo 55» nos han puesto en la vanguardia mundial en cuanto a políticas vinculantes en medioambiente, energía y clima. Los socialistas españoles vemos con preocupación el viraje hacia el negacionismo climático del Partido Popular Europeo, ya que valida los argumentos populistas de la extrema derecha, rompe el consenso en torno al Pacto Verde Europeo y pone en peligro la existencia de las generaciones futuras y del planeta. Celebramos que la ofensiva del PPE contra la Ley de Restauración de la Naturaleza, pieza clave del Pacto Verde Europeo, no saliera adelante y esperamos que recapacite su posición.
translated_text: Climate change and environmental degradation are the greatest threats to our well-being and our existence. Given the climate emergency we find ourselves in, the Socialist Group has been committed to the European Green Deal from the outset, and we believe it is the only possible development strategy to guarantee a decent future. Measures such as the European Climate Law and the "Target 55" package have put us at the global forefront of binding policies on the environment, energy, and climate. We Spanish Socialists view with concern the shift towards climate denialism by the European People's Party, as it validates the populist arguments of the far right, breaks the consensus around the European Green Deal, and endangers the existence of future generations and the planet. We welcome the fact that the EPP's offensive against the Nature Restoration Law, a key part of the European Green Deal, did not succeed, and we hope that it will reconsider its position.

speaker: Guido Reil
agenda: 19. Delivering on the Green Deal: risk of compromising the EU path to the green transition and its international commitments (debate)
text: Die EU präsentiert ihren Grünen Deal als eine sogenannte grüne Wirtschaftsstrategie und als Vorbild für den Rest der Welt. Dies ist eine Lüge, die aufhören muss. Weltweit sind die CO2-Emissionen stetig gestiegen und sie werden auch weiterhin steigen. Aufgrund des Bevölkerungswachstums und des Strebens nach materiellem Wohlstand wird der Energieverbrauch auf der Erde weiter steigen. Fossile Energien bleiben dabei die wichtigste Säule. China eröffnet jede Woche zwei neue Kohlekraftwerke. Indien treibt seine wirtschaftliche Entwicklung weiter voran, wie die gesamte Welt außerhalb der EU. Die Reduktion der CO2-Emissionen innerhalb der EU wurde und wird durch den Anstieg der Emissionen in vielen anderen Teilen der Welt mehr als ausgeglichen. Tatsache ist, dass das Ziel des Pariser Abkommens nicht erreicht wird. Glaubt man wirklich, dass sich Länder wie China oder Indien ihre Wirtschaftspolitik von der EU diktieren lassen? Einige Fakten und Daten: Selbst wenn das schlimmste Erwärmungsszenario des Weltklimarats eintreten würde, wären die wirtschaftlichen Folgen vernachlässigbar. Der Weltklimarat schätzt, dass eine Erwärmung um drei Grad zu einer Verminderung des weltweiten Wirtschaftswachstums um 0,04 Prozent pro Jahr bis zum Jahr 2100 führen kann, was kaum messbar ist. In anderen Worten: Das Wirtschaftswachstum und der Wohlstand in Europa werden viel mehr durch die EU-Klimapolitik als durch die globale Erwärmung bedroht.
translated_text: The EU presents its Green Deal as a so-called green economic strategy and a model for the rest of the world. This is a lie that must stop. CO2 emissions have risen steadily worldwide and will continue to do so. Due to population growth and the pursuit of material prosperity, energy consumption on Earth will continue to rise. Fossil fuels remain the most important pillar in this regard. China opens two new coal-fired power plants every week. India continues to drive its economic development, as does the entire world outside the EU. The reduction in CO2 emissions within the EU has been and continues to be more than offset by the increase in emissions in many other parts of the world. The fact is that the goal of the Paris Agreement will not be achieved. Do we really believe that countries such as China or India will allow the EU to dictate their economic policy? Some facts and figures: Even if the worst-case warming scenario predicted by the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change were to occur, the economic consequences would be negligible. The IPCC estimates that a three-degree rise in temperature could lead to a 0.04 percent reduction in global economic growth per year until 2100, which is barely measurable. In other words, economic growth and prosperity in Europe are much more threatened by EU climate policy than by global warming.

speaker: Christian Ehler
agenda: 4. A Green Deal Industrial Plan for the Net-Zero Age (debate)
text: Madam President, ladies and gentlemen, in 2020, the Commission proudly announced a new industrial policy to implement the European Green Deal as a growth strategy. Yet it took a loud bang from the US to wake up the von der Leyen Commission. Three years later, they finally woke up to the fact that simply placing Fit for 55 regulation is not enough. We need a business case for the European industry that the industrial transition will be a success. The EPP Group welcomes, therefore, the Green Deal Industrial plan, but the EPP Group really wants to make sure that the von der Leyen Commission hears this final warning shot loud and clear. So far, the Commission approach has to be too prescriptive, too ideological, and too much focused on regulating the demand side of the European market. Technology neutrality was an afterthought rather than a leading principle. What we need is a serious effort to create a regulatory framework that gives our industry certainty and freedom to innovate and invest. This requires simple, fast, predictable decisions on state aid and permitting, for example, and domestic sourcing of raw materials. Productivity is our industry’s competitive advantage, not even mentioned in the statement from the Commission. Our social and energy costs are among the highest in the world, but so is also the quality of our scientists and engineers. We need to address these high costs, but most importantly, we need to unleash the potential of our innovation—driven productivity growth. To ensure our competitiveness on the global market we also need an ambitious free trade agenda, and yes, we want to ratify the pending trade agreements. If the von der Leyen Commission realises what needs to happen, the European economy can thrive and provide quality jobs for our citizens also in the future. And only if our economy thrives, the European Green Deal can be a success.
translated_text: Madam President, ladies and gentlemen, in 2020, the Commission proudly announced a new industrial policy to implement the European Green Deal as a growth strategy. Yet it took a loud bang from the US to wake up the von der Leyen Commission. Three years later, they finally woke up to the fact that simply placing Fit for 55 regulation is not enough. We need a business case for the European industry that the industrial transition will be a success. The EPP Group welcomes, therefore, the Green Deal Industrial plan, but the EPP Group really wants to make sure that the von der Leyen Commission hears this final warning shot loud and clear. So far, the Commission approach has to be too prescriptive, too ideological, and too much focused on regulating the demand side of the European market. Technology neutrality was an afterthought rather than a leading principle. What we need is a serious effort to create a regulatory framework that gives our industry certainty and freedom to innovate and invest. This requires simple, fast, predictable decisions on state aid and permitting, for example, and domestic sourcing of raw materials. Productivity is our industry’s competitive advantage, not even mentioned in the statement from the Commission. Our social and energy costs are among the highest in the world, but so is also the quality of our scientists and engineers. We need to address these high costs, but most importantly, we need to unleash the potential of our innovation—driven productivity growth. To ensure our competitiveness on the global market we also need an ambitious free trade agenda, and yes, we want to ratify the pending trade agreements. If the von der Leyen Commission realises what needs to happen, the European economy can thrive and provide quality jobs for our citizens also in the future. And only if our economy thrives, the European Green Deal can be a success.

speaker: Iratxe García Pérez
agenda: 4. A Green Deal Industrial Plan for the Net-Zero Age (debate)
text: Señora presidenta, queridos compañeros, la carrera de subsidios ha comenzado. Estados Unidos, China, Japón o India están en la misma competición. Es positivo que países aliados como Estados Unidos apuesten por la transición hacia un mundo sin emisiones. Pero la Unión Europea no puede permitir caminos desleales y que vulneren las normas de la Organización Mundial del Comercio. Ha llegado la hora de reaccionar. Nos hemos quedado atrás en sectores como la fabricación de microchips o el mundo digital. Ni un solo de los gigantes digitales o de las grandes redes sociales es europeo. El Plan Industrial del Pacto Verde presentado por la Comisión Europea no puede ser una oportunidad perdida. Su actual planteamiento ignora los derechos de la clase trabajadora y su financiación está en un limbo. Debemos aprovechar el momento decisivo de la revolución verde y digital para modernizar nuestra economía. Lo más urgente es una reforma del mercado eléctrico, porque su actual diseño es la principal desventaja de la industria europea. También necesitamos una revisión del modelo de ayudas públicas a las empresas que nos permita un despliegue acelerado de los proyectos estratégicos en el marco de los planes nacionales de recuperación y en sectores que garanticen una mayor autonomía estratégica, tecnológica y energética, como las energías limpias, los semiconductores y los vehículos eléctricos. Pero la revisión de las ayudas de Estado no solo requiere una modificación de los umbrales, sino un instrumento que equilibre las diferencias de tamaño y margen fiscal de los Estados miembros. Bajo ningún concepto podemos permitir que la carrera global de subsidios se convierta en una carrera interna entre los Estados miembros que fragmente nuestro mercado único. Otro paso debe ser la adopción de la financiación verde para movilizar la inversión pública y privada necesaria. El apoyo a los presupuestos nacionales puede prestarse con reglas fiscales adecuadas en un renovado Pacto de estabilidad y crecimiento a través de la movilización de un instrumento financiero inspirado en el programa SURE. El Plan Industrial del Pacto Verde solo será una realidad si se protege el corazón que bombea la industria europea desde hace dos siglos: la clase trabajadora. Sin trabajadores y trabajadoras no hay industria. Cualquier empresa que reciba ayuda pública debe impulsar los derechos laborales, salarios decentes, condiciones de trabajo dignas, negociación colectiva y derecho sindical. Señorías, el objetivo prioritario del Plan Industrial del Pacto Verde debe ser competir de manera eficaz con Estados Unidos y China, no entre Estados miembros dentro del mercado único. Y no hay mejor manera de conmemorar el trigésimo aniversario del mercado único que contribuir al nacimiento de una industria europea competitiva, sostenible, digital y que respete la dignidad de la clase trabajadora que merece.
translated_text: Madam President, dear colleagues, the subsidy race has begun. The United States, China, Japan, and India are all in the same competition. It is positive that allied countries such as the United States are committed to the transition to a zero-emission world. But the European Union cannot allow unfair practices that violate World Trade Organization rules. The time has come to react. We have fallen behind in sectors such as microchip manufacturing and the digital world. Not a single one of the digital giants or large social networks is European. The Green Deal Industrial Plan presented by the European Commission cannot be a missed opportunity. Its current approach ignores the rights of the working class and its financing is in limbo. We must seize the decisive moment of the green and digital revolution to modernize our economy. The most urgent task is to reform the electricity market, because its current design is the main disadvantage for European industry. We also need a review of the model of public aid to companies that will allow us to accelerate the deployment of strategic projects within the framework of national recovery plans and in sectors that guarantee greater strategic, technological, and energy autonomy, such as clean energy, semiconductors, and electric vehicles. But the review of state aid requires not only a modification of the thresholds, but also an instrument that balances the differences in size and fiscal space between Member States. Under no circumstances can we allow the global subsidy race to become an internal race between Member States that fragments our single market. Another step must be the adoption of green financing to mobilize the necessary public and private investment. Support for national budgets can be provided with appropriate fiscal rules in a renewed Stability and Growth Pact through the mobilization of a financial instrument inspired by the SURE program. The Green Deal's Industrial Plan will only become a reality if the heart that has been pumping European industry for two centuries is protected: the working class.
